response.justification: Candidate has strong geospatial/GIS domain experience (founder of GISCollective, work with Green Map) and exposure to high-throughput distributed systems (Sociomantic), which align well with the routing tiling problem space. However, the primary required skill — modern C++ or Rust — is a significant gap: C/C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no Rust experience. The candidate's recent and primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Worth a screening call to probe systems-level depth and willingness to ramp on C++, but not a strong technical match on the core requirement.
strengths: Deep geospatial/GIS experience including founding an open-source geo-data platform
Distributed systems exposure handling millions of HTTP req/sec at Sociomantic with low-latency async I/O
Demonstrated polyglot adaptability and comfort working across the full stack
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (~6 months in 2014-2015); no Rust experience
Recent career trajectory is web/product (TypeScript, Node.js), not systems programming
No explicit navigation/routing or production map data pipeline experience
